claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child - Alex, who's 16 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 16 years old, I need to ask an important question:

Is Alex still in approved education or training (such as school, college, or an approved training course)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education - studying for A-levels at sixth form.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great! Now I need to ask about your residency status:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you the person who is responsible for Alex - meaning you live with them and/or pay for their upkeep (such as food, clothes, pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for all their needs - food, clothes, everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One final question:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Although Alex is 16, Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training
- Alex is studying for A-levels at sixth form, which qualifies as approved education
- You live in the UK and are responsible for Alex, who lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. 

**Important note:** You should be aware of the High Income Child Benefit tax charge - if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay a tax charge. You'll also need to inform HMRC if Alex's circumstances change, such as if they leave education or start working 24 hours or more per week.

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and is in approved education (studying for A-levels at sixth form). Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, but you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex (lives with them and provides for their needs), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 16-year-old child Alex. Although Alex is 16, they are still in approved education (sixth form A-levels), which means Child Benefit can continue until Alex is under 20 as long as they remain in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex who lives with them, and no one else is claiming for this child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 16 years old and is in approved education (studying for A-levels at sixth form). Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, but you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex (lives with them and provides for their needs), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 16-year-old child Alex. Although Alex is 16, they are still in approved education (sixth form A-levels), which means Child Benefit can continue until Alex is under 20 as long as they remain in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex who lives with them, and no one else is claiming for this child.
